evidence: `useButtonGroupProps` rebuilds nearly identical shared fields (`isVideo`, `readOnly`, `isSpecialEditMode`, `selectedProjectId`, `isCloudMode`) across four builder functions, then `LightboxLayout` consumes large spread prop bags.

`useImageEditValue` keeps both a new structured model and a legacy flat param surface with a full adapter (`toModelsFromLegacy`), while `useInlineEditState` already builds the structured model, leaving dual boundary shapes in the same seam.

Travel task builders duplicate low-level payload parsing/normalization helpers (`toRecordOrEmpty`, numeric/string coercion, legacy field stripping patterns) across modules instead of one boundary translation layer.

findings.summary: Lightbox control seams duplicate shared prop translation, increasing glue-code drift risk.
evidence: `useButtonGroupProps` has four builders that each restate the same shared context fields before appending local inputs.
`useVideoLightboxRenderModel` assembles multiple large memoized prop objects, which are then forwarded into layout/controls.
`LightboxLayout` consumes broad spread objects (`buttonGroupProps`, `workflowBarProps`) rather than narrower per-control contracts.
findings.suggestion: Create a single typed `LightboxControlContext` object and let each control read shared fields from context/hook, passing only control-specific props at call sites. Remove repeated shared-field copying builders.

findings.summary: Inline edit seam maintains both legacy and structured interfaces, keeping integration contracts ambiguous.
evidence: `useImageEditValue` accepts `UseImageEditValueModels | UseImageEditValueLegacyParams` and contains a full field-by-field legacy adapter.
`useInlineEditState` already constructs the structured `UseImageEditValueModels` via `buildImageEditValueModels`, so one seam still supports two contracts.
`InlineEditView` depends on a very broad state object, amplifying the cost of keeping both model shapes alive.
findings.suggestion: Deprecate the legacy flat param signature: make `useImageEditValue` accept only `UseImageEditValueModels`, delete `UseImageEditValueLegacyParams`/`toModelsFromLegacy`, and migrate remaining callers in one pass.

findings.summary: Task payload translation logic is fragmented across task modules instead of a single boundary mapper.
evidence: Both travel payload modules define similar local coercion helpers (`toRecordOrEmpty`, numeric/string coercers) and manually rebuild payload shape rules.
`taskParamsUtils` separately re-derives orchestrator/segment fields via its own parsing logic, creating another translation path for similar data.
The same domain fields (segment/orchestrator payload, prompt/image extraction) are interpreted in multiple places with independent fallback order.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a shared `taskPayloadBoundary` module for typed decode/normalize/encode of orchestrator + segment params, and make both builders and readers consume that single translator API.
